如果你听说过生酮饮食，一种极低碳水化合物的饮食方式，你可能会把它与争议和另一种肥胖症治疗方法联系在一起。
媒体上的个人轶事讲述了对抗肥胖症和2型糖尿病的惊人效果。但这项研究没有定论，我们对这种饮食的长期影响知之甚少。许多专业人士警告不要吃脂肪含量太高的饮食。
然而，研究人员对这种极端高脂肪饮食的兴趣与日俱增。仅在2019年，就写了一千多篇关于这个话题的新研究文章。
奥斯陆大学医院的研究员兼临床营养学家MagnHild Kverneland说，这些研究不再只关注肥胖和糖尿病。
她说：“研究人员正在研究诸如帕金森氏症、阿尔茨海默氏症、肌萎缩侧索硬化症、脑瘤、偏头痛和多发性硬化症等疾病。”
生酮饮食实际上从20世纪20年代就开始用于癫痫的治疗。
已经知道，如果患者生活在非常严格的饮食中，高达90%的卡路里来自脂肪，而碳水化合物每天的摄入量不到20克，那么他们的癫痫发作通常会明显减少。
但是，与正常饮食如此不同的饮食是很难遵循的。因此，当治疗这种疾病的有效药物开始出现时，用饮食作为治疗方法就不再流行了。
几十年来，总共开发了25种不同的抗癫痫药物。但研究也表明，这些药物并不是对每个人都有效。
“大约三分之一的患者从药物中得不到可接受的缓解程度。这是一个真正的挑战，“Kverneland说。
这就是为什么研究人员和治疗师们又恢复了旧的饮食养生法。2008年，“柳叶刀神经学”杂志发表了一项突破性的研究，研究对象是那些严重癫痫不能通过药物控制的儿童。
“研究表明，生酮饮食比继续正常治疗有更好的效果，”Kverneland说。
现在，这种类型的治疗是挪威为患者提供的治疗的一部分，在儿童得不到足够药物帮助的情况下。这种疗法也越来越多地用于成年人。
Kverneland自己也研究了这种饮食对患有癫痫的成年人是如何起作用的。研究表明，在参与者中，生酮饮食与较少的癫痫发作有关。
然而，影响是温和的，而且人与人之间存在很大的差异。一些患者表现出明显的改善，而一些患者实际上变得更糟了。
这些不同结果背后的原因可能是癫痫本身是一种非常多样化的疾病，有很多可能的原因。以及Kverneland和他的同事在去年发表在Epilepsa杂志上的一项研究中发现的另一个令人着迷的事实：
她说：我们发现生酮饮食影响了成年患者血液中抗癫痫药物的水平。
不同药物的效果可能不同，但饮食确实似乎降低了至少其中一些药物的效果。
她说：“对于医生来说，记住这一点很重要，因为这可能也适用于其他医学药物。”
研究人员还不确定为什么生酮饮食会有这种效果，但她怀疑高脂肪饮食可能会影响肝脏的血液循环率，而肝脏正是药物分解的地方。
它提醒我们，当我们从正常饮食转变为生酮饮食时，体内实际上正在发生戏剧性的变化，脂肪摄入量增加了一倍或三倍。
当你停止进食碳水化合物-通常从面包、米饭、土豆、根茎蔬菜、水果、豆类和糖果中获得大量碳水化合物-身体就失去了主要的能量来源-葡萄糖。它是葡萄糖，也就是血糖，通常是身体细胞的燃料。
当你摄入的碳水化合物都用完了，你的身体就像一辆行驶在高速公路中央的汽车，油箱几乎是空的。
事实证明，大自然为这样的情况提供了条件。它的车身实际上有点像混合动力车。它有两个平行的能量系统--一个依靠葡萄糖，另一个依靠脂肪。因此，当葡萄糖系统耗尽燃料时，脂肪系统就会接手。
当身体从食物中耗尽葡萄糖时，它开始消耗储存的脂肪。脂肪酸从体内的脂肪细胞中释放出来，转化为酮体。细胞可以用这些酮代替葡萄糖作为能源。
“这是一种自然条件，对我们物种的生存起到了推波助澜的作用。从进化的角度看，我们已经很好地适应了这种情况，“Kverneland说。
当我们的祖先在狩猎或采集水果或其他野生食物时运气不佳，他们不得不忍受几天，甚至几周没有食物。他们仍然需要能够进行身体和精神上的操作，这样他们才能最终获得所需的食物。
“人体将酮体视为超级燃料。它们每单位比葡萄糖提供更多的能量，“Kverneland说。
与此同时，身体进行了许多改变，以使这种替代能源系统发挥作用。
从葡萄糖系统切换到脂肪系统的人在最初的几天或几周内会感到相当疲惫，这是很常见的。但之后身体就会适应新的燃料系统并正常工作。
此外，似乎身体在这种转变中所做的改变可以对某些疾病产生积极的影响。
我们对酮体的许多了解都是相当新的。但是人类很早就知道饥饿和禁食对你的健康有积极的影响。
唯一的问题是，禁食并不是治疗长期疾病的一种有吸引力的方法。这就是生酮饮食的用武之地。
当你切断身体的碳水化合物供应，而只让脂肪燃烧时，即使你没有挨饿，它也会切换到替代能源系统。
“生酮饮食令人兴奋的是它模仿了身体对禁食的反应，”Kverneland说。
正是这个想法导致研究人员在20世纪初将生酮饮食作为癫痫的一种治疗方法进行了试验。事实证明，这个假设是正确的。
近年来，随着我们更多地了解到当身体以脂肪为主要燃料时会发生什么，研究人员想知道：其他疾病可以用这种特殊的饮食来治疗吗？
研究人员对这种治疗方法越来越感兴趣的领域之一是阿尔茨海默氏病。
先前的研究表明，阿尔茨海默氏症患者在脑细胞利用葡萄糖的过程中存在问题。继续汽车的比喻：发动机不能再正常吸收汽油了。
但也许燃烧脂肪的能量系统还在运转呢？如果是这样的话，它能否通过替代能源系统和超级燃料酮体提供足够的能量来为脑细胞提供燃料？
一些小规模的研究已经完成，轻度或中度阿尔茨海默病患者遵循生酮饮食或服用含有酮体的补充剂。根据2018年发表在“神经治疗学”杂志上的一篇综述，这种方法对症状显示了积极的效果。
但这种治疗并不是对每个人都有效，可能是因为我们有不同的基因。研究人员需要做更多更大规模的研究，才能确定任何事情。
根据神经治疗学综述，包括ALS在内的几种神经疾病也有有趣的结果。
肌萎缩侧索硬化症，或肌萎缩侧索硬化症，与身体能量转换系统的紊乱以及大脑和神经系统对葡萄糖的吸收有关。动物研究表明，生酮饮食可以减缓或改善症状。
其他研究表明，生酮饮食也可能有助于多发性硬化症和帕金森氏症。
根据神经治疗学评论，几项研究表明，生酮饮食可以帮助偏头痛和丛集性头痛的人。
然而，在这种情况下，能源供应很可能不是问题。癫痫和偏头痛同时出现症状的原因之一是神经细胞往往太容易发出信号。
以前的研究表明，酮体似乎调节神经递质-细胞用来将信号从一个神经细胞发送到另一个神经细胞的物质。
根据2017年发表在“神经科学”杂志上的一篇综述，一些关于人类的研究表明，生酮饮食可以对偏头痛发作产生快速效果。
但是文档远远不够好。在这方面也需要更多的研究，以显示这种饮食是否对病人有效，以及它在身体中实际是如何起作用的。
最近，生酮饮食被证明有效果的一个领域也是如此--癌症。
研究人员知道，许多不同的癌细胞依赖于葡萄糖作为能源。
从那时起，下一步是显而易见的：你能通过去除癌症的燃料来源来饿死它，同时用来自脂肪的能量保持身体健康细胞的存活吗？
已经对细胞和动物进行了几项研究，表明生酮饮食可以有显著的效果，特别是与化疗和放射治疗等传统治疗相结合时。
研究人员在2019年发表在《分子代谢》杂志上的一篇综述中写道，这种饮食似乎增强了一些癌症治疗的效果，并保护患者免受一些癌症患者经历的严重体重减轻的影响。
研究人员得出结论，到目前为止的研究结果表明，生酮饮食可以帮助治疗各种癌症。
然而，几位癌症医生此前曾警告癌症患者停止这种极端的饮食。他们担心限制饮食会使生活变得困难，患者会难以摄入足够的食物。
我们也不知道这种饮食可能对哪些类型的癌症有帮助。也有研究表明没有任何效果，一些动物研究表明，这种饮食甚至会加剧癌症。
总而言之，现在就断言生酮饮食在不同疾病的治疗中可能起到什么作用，以及它对身体有什么影响，还为时过早。
例如，一些研究表明，生酮饮食可以抑制炎症，而炎症通常是各种疾病的标志。
其他研究表明，特殊饮食会导致肠道菌群的变化。许多研究表明，肠道微生物在各种疾病中起着重要作用。
饮食的改变可能看起来很平凡，因为它们与食物有关，而且是我们自己可以做的事情。但将生酮饮食视为一种药物可能更合适。
这是一种有医学效果的治疗方法。但要遵循这一点可能会非常苛刻。此外，这种饮食可能有副作用，在最坏的情况下可能是危险的。
例如，众所周知，生酮饮食会导致消化问题，增加低密度脂蛋白(LDL)胆固醇，这与心脏病的风险更高有关。
根据2016年发表在“神经生物学老化”杂志上的一项研究，当挪威研究人员让细胞能量周转受损的小鼠接受生酮饮食时，这些动物的病情实际上在治疗后变得更糟。
就像任何药物一样，生酮饮食的作用因人而异。
Kverneland说，生酮饮食对癫痫的影响有很大的个体差异。当挪威的患者尝试这种饮食时，他们会得到彻底的指导和随访。
她说：“我们会验血，并监测疗效和副作用。”“有些人看到了真正令人惊叹的效果。那么每个人都很开心。但每个病人的反应是不同的。“。
“如果我们能预测到谁会看到效果，那就太好了。我希望我们能在这方面做更多的研究。“她说。
I.D.Meira等人：生酮饮食和癫痫：我们到目前为止所知道的，神经科学前沿，2019年1月。
D.D.Weber等人：癌症治疗中的生酮饮食--我们的立场是什么？分子新陈代谢，2020年3月。总结。
M.Kverneland等人：改良的阿特金斯饮食和抗癫痫药物在患有抗药性癫痫的成人中的药代动力学相互作用，癫痫，2019年11月。总结。
M.Kverneland等人：改良阿特金斯饮食对成人耐药灶性癫痫的影响：一项随机临床试验，癫痫，2018年6月。总结。
T.J.W.McDonald，M.C.Cervenka：成人神经疾病的生酮饮食，神经治疗学，2018年10月。总结。
P.Barbanti等人：偏头痛的生酮饮食：理论基础、发现和前景，神经科学，2017年5月。总结。
K.H.Lauritzen等人：生酮饮食加速小鼠的神经退化，并在前脑诱导线粒体DNA毒性，神经生物学of Aging，2016年12月。总结。
note_en:
If you have heard of the ketogenic diet, an extreme low carb approach to eating, you may associate it with controversy and an alternative treatment for obesity.
 Personal anecdotes in the media tell about astounding effects against obesity and type 2 diabetes. But the research is inconclusive, and we know very little about long term effects of such a diet. Many professionals warn against a diet which is so high in fat.
 Nevertheless, there is a growing interest in this extreme high-fat diet among researchers. In 2019 alone, more than a thousand new research articles were written on the topic.
 And these studies are no longer solely focused on obesity and diabetes, says researcher and clinical nutritionist Magnhild Kverneland at Oslo University Hospital.
 “Researchers are looking at diseases such as Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, ALS, brain tumours, migraines and multiple sclerosis,” she said.
    The ketogenic diet has actually been used in the treatment of epilepsy since the 1920s.
 It had been known that patients often had significantly fewer seizures if they lived on a very strict diet, where up to 90 per cent of calories came from fat and carbohydrate intake was less than 20 grams a day.
 But a diet that is so different from normal is hard to follow. So when effective medicines began to emerge for the disease, using diet as a treatment went out of fashion.
 Over the decades, a total of 25 different anti-epilepsy drugs have been developed. But research has also shown that these medicines do not work for everyone.
 “About a third of patients don’t get an acceptable level of relief from the medicines. That’s a real challenge,” Kverneland said.
  This is why researchers and therapists have brought back the old diet regimen. In 2008, the journal The Lancet Neurology published a ground-breaking study of children whose severe epilepsy couldn’t be controlled with medication.
 “The study showed that the ketogenic diet had a much better effect than continuing with treatment as normal,” says Kverneland.
 Now this type of treatment is part of what patients are offered in Norway, in situations where children don’t get enough help from medicines. The treatment is also increasingly used in adults.
 Kverneland herself has studied how the diet works for adults with epilepsy.  The study showed that a ketogenic diet was associated with fewer seizures in participants.
 However, the impact was moderate, and there was a big difference from person to person. Some patients showed significant improvement, while some actually got worse.
 The reason behind these varied results is likely that epilepsy itself is a very varied disease, with many possible causes. As well as another fascinating fact which Kverneland and collegues discovered in a study  which was published in the journal Epilepsa last year:
   "We found that the ketogenic diet affected the level of anti-epileptic drugs in the blood of adult patients," she said.
 The effect is probably different for different drugs, but the diet did appear to reduce the effect for at least some of them.
 “It’s important for doctors to keep this in mind, because it may also be true for other medical drugs,” she said.
 The researcher is not yet sure why the ketogenic diet has this effect, but she suspects a high-fat diet is likely to affect the blood circulation rate in the liver, which is where medicines are broken down.
 And it's a reminder of the dramatic changes that are actually happening in the body when we switch from a normal diet to a ketogenic diet, where fat intake is doubled or tripled.
  When you stop eating carbohydrates – which you usually get in large amounts from bread, rice, potatoes, root vegetables, fruits, beans and sweets – the body loses its main source of energy, glucose. It is glucose – blood sugar – which usually fuels your body’s cells.
 When all the carbohydrates you have consumed are gone, your body is like a car in the middle of the highway, where the fuel tank is nearly empty.
  Turns out nature provides for situations like this. The body is actually built a bit like a hybrid car. It has two parallel energy systems – one that runs on glucose, and one that runs on fat. So when the glucose system runs out of fuel, the fat system takes over.
   As the body runs out of glucose from food, it begins to consume its fat stores. Fatty acids are released from the fat cells in the body and are converted into ketone bodies. Cells can use these ketones as an energy source, instead of glucose.
 “This is a natural condition that has contributed to the survival of our species. Evolutionally, we are well adapted to it,” says Kverneland.
 When our ancestors had bad luck hunting or gathering fruits or other wild foods, they had to endure days, maybe weeks without food. They still needed to be able to operate both physically and mentally so that they could eventually get the food they needed.
   “The body sees ketone bodies like a super fuel. They provide more energy per unit than glucose,” says Kverneland.
 At the same time, the body makes many changes to allow this alternative energy system to work.
 It is common for people who switch from the glucose system to the fat system to feel quite tired during the first days or weeks. But then the body gets used to the new fuel system and works normally.
 In addition, it appears that the changes the body makes during this shift can have positive effects on some diseases.
   Much of what we know about ketone bodies is quite new. But humans have long known that hunger and fasting can have a positive effect on your health.
  The only problem is that fasting is not an attractive way to treat a long-term illness. That’s where the ketogenic diet comes in.
 When you turn off the body’s supply of carbohydrates and instead only give it fat to burn, it will switch over to the alternative energy system, even if you aren’t starving.
 “What’s exciting about the ketogenic diet is that it mimics the body's reaction to fasting,” says Kverneland.
 It was precisely this idea that led researchers to test the ketogenic diet as a treatment for epilepsy, in the early 1900s. And it turned out that the hypothesis was correct.
 And in recent years, as we have learned more about what happens when the body uses fat as its primary fuel, researchers have wondered: Can other diseases be treated with this special diet?
  One of the areas where researchers are increasingly interested in this approach to treatment is Alzheimer's disease.
  Previous research has suggested that people with Alzheimer's have problems with the processes that allow glucose to be used by brain cells. To continue with the car analogy: The engine can no longer take in gasoline properly.
 But maybe the energy system that burns fat still works? If so, could it provide enough energy to fuel brain cells via the alternative energy system and the super-fuel ketone bodies?
 A few small studies have been done where people with mild or moderate Alzheimer's have followed a ketogenic diet or taken supplements containing ketone bodies. The approach has shown positive effects on symptoms, according to  a review published in the journal Neurotherapeutics in 2018.
 But the treatment did not work for everyone, perhaps because we have different genes. Researchers need to do more and larger studies before they can say anything for sure.
  There have also been interesting results for several neurological disorders, including ALS, according to the Neurotherapeutics review.
 ALS, or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, is linked to disorders in the body’s energy conversion system and uptake of glucose by the brain and nervous system. Animal studies have suggested that a ketogenic diet may slow down or improve symptoms.
 Other studies have suggested that a ketogenic diet might also help with multiple sclerosis and Parkinson's disease.
   According to the Neurotherapeutics review, several studies have shown that a ketogenic diet can help people who get migraines and cluster headaches.
 In this situation, however, it’s likely that energy supply isn’t the problem. One of the reasons for symptoms in both epilepsy and migraines is that nerve cells tend to send signals too easily.
 Previous studies have shown that ketone bodies appear to regulate neurotransmitters — the substances that cells use to send signals from one nerve cell to the next.
 Some studies on humans suggest that the ketogenic diet can have a rapid effect on migraine attacks, according to a  2017 review published in the journal Neurological Sciences.
 But the documentation is far from good enough. Here too, more studies are needed, to both show whether the diet actually works on sick people, and how it actually works in the body.
 The same is true of a recent area where ketogenic diets have been shown to have an effect — cancer.
  Researchers know that a number of different cancer cells depend on glucose as an energy source.
 From there, the next step is obvious: Could you starve a cancerous tumour by taking away its fuel source, while keeping the body’s healthy cells alive with energy from fats?
 Several studies have been done on cells and animals suggesting that a ketogenic diet can have a significant effect, especially in combination with traditional treatments such as chemotherapy and radiation therapy.
  The diet appears to enhance the effect of some cancer treatments, as well as protect patients from the severe weight loss that some cancer patients experience, researchers wrote in a  review published in Molecular Metabolism in 2019.
 The researchers concluded that the findings to date suggest the ketogenic diet can help with a variety of cancers.
 However, several cancer doctors have previously warned cancer patients to stop with this extreme diet. They are concerned that the restrictive diet will make life difficult, and that patients will have trouble consuming enough food.
 We also do not know which types of cancer the diet might help with. There are also studies that show no effect, and some studies in animals have indicated that the diet can even aggravate cancer.
  All in all, it's too early to say for sure what role the ketogenic diet could play in the treatment of different diseases, and what effects it has on the body.
  For example, some studies have shown that a ketogenic diet can curb inflammation, which is often a hallmark of a variety of diseases.
 Other studies show that the special diet leads to changes in the gut flora. Much research has suggested that the gut microorganisms play an important role in various diseases.
  Dietary changes can seem mundane, because they concern food and are something we can do on our own. But it may be more appropriate to think of the ketogenic diet as a drug.
 It’s a treatment that has medical effects. But it can be very demanding to follow. In addition, the diet can have side effects that in the worst case can be dangerous.
 For example, it is known that the ketogenic diet can cause digestive problems and increase LDL cholesterol, which is linked to a higher risk of heart disease.
 And when Norwegian researchers put mice, whose cells' energy turnover had been damaged, on a ketogenic diet, the animals actually got worse from the treatment,  according to a 2016 study in the journal Neurobiological Aging.
 And much like any drug, the ketogenic diet will work differently from person to person.
 Kverneland says there are large individual differences in the effect of the ketogenic diet on epilepsy. When patients in Norway try the diet, they receive thorough guidance and follow-up.
 “We take blood tests and monitor the effects and side effects,” she said. “Some see a truly amazing effect. Then everyone is happy. But the response is different from patient to patient.”
 “What would be nice would be if we could predict who is going to see an effect. It's something I hope we can do more research on,” she said.
   I. D. Meira, et al:  Ketogenic Diet and Epilepsy: What We Know So Far, Frontiers in Neuroscience, January 2019.
 D. D. Weber, et al: Ketogenic diet in the treatment of cancer – Where do we stand? Molecular Metabolism, March 2020.  Summary.
 M. Kverneland et al: Pharmacokinetic Interaction Between Modified Atkins Diet and Antiepileptic Drugs in Adults With Drug-Resistant Epilepsy, Epilepsia, November 2019.  Summary.
 M. Kverneland, et al: Effect of Modified Atkins Diet in Adults With Drug-Resistant Focal Epilepsy: A Randomized Clinical Trial, Epilepsia, June 2018.  Summary.
 T. J. W. McDonald, M. C. Cervenka: Ketogenic Diets for Adult Neurological Disorders, Neurotherapeutics, October 2018.  Summary.
 P. Barbanti et al: Ketogenic Diet in Migraine: Rationale, Findings and Perspectives, Neurological Science, May 2017. Summary.
 K. H. Lauritzen, et al: A ketogenic diet accelerates neurodegeneration in mice with induced mitochondrial DNA toxicity in the forebrain, Neurobiology of Aging, December 2016.  Summary.

title: Pavel Durov想要一项法律，让苹果允许iPhone用户安装其他应用商店
title_en: Pavel Durov wants a law to make Apple allow iPhone users install other app store

note:
像任何其他软件开发人员一样，阿尔瓦罗·贾斯滕过去醒着的大部分时间都是在屏幕前度过的，他坐在椅子上四处走动，同时编写代码，处理数据库和处理电子表格。但自从第一批新冠肺炎病例在他的祖国巴西萌芽以来，贾斯滕的日常生活发生了重大变化。他现在全职工作，没有报酬，这曾经是一个小的附带项目，现在已经成为衡量大流行对他国家影响的全面来源。
同事和推特追随者都知道@turicas在2018年创立了他的非营利性组织Brasil.IO，旨在让巴西的公共数据更容易获得。现在，他正专注于关于该国这种新型冠状病毒的信息(或缺乏信息)，该国是大流行的中心之一。
“如果我醒着，很可能是在工地上工作。周六、周日、节假日，“他告诉世界其他地区。他以一种用户友好的格式发布名单-该国公职或企业主的候选人-开始了他的网站。
目前，该网站的新冠肺炎系列以独立的每日监测州和市的细分案例为特色。40多名志愿开发人员和数据爱好者参与该项目，收集更新、识别模式并创建数据集。
收集数据可能是一项困难的手动任务。志愿者们的工作范围覆盖了巴西5000多个城市。贾斯滕解释说：“有时候，数据只在内部系统中可用，我们必须请求它。”或者数据可用，但格式不容易处理。挑战在于如何将这些海量的信息--可以是大的电子表格或松散的PDF格式--转换成更轻便的文件或用户友好的表格和图形。
到2018年，数据透明度在巴西已经走过了很长一段路。自1985年军事统治结束以来，该国见证了一些突破，比如2011年批准了类似于美国信息自由法(Freedom Of Information Act)的《信息获取法》(Access Of Information Law)。
但像巴西开放知识组织(Open Knowledge Brasil)执行董事费尔南达·坎帕努奇(Fernanda Campagnucci)这样的专家表示，数据透明度并不是博尔索纳罗政府的优先事项。相反，它似乎更喜欢一种不言而喻的政策，隐藏、质疑或删除任何与其叙述不符的数据。她告诉世界其他地区：“一种限制获取[信息]的姿态正在取得进展，这不是巴西的典型做法。”
这些努力是从去年开始的。2019年4月，博尔索纳罗政府在毒品信息网站上关闭了巴西观察站，该网站显示了人口中非法使用毒品的数量。后来，在8月份，巴西国家空间研究所所长因显示亚马逊7月前两周的森林砍伐与去年同期相比增加了68%而受到抨击而被解雇。今年早些时候，政府削减了全国人口普查的预算，这导致了一项将人口调查问题减少近三分之一的提议。
冠状病毒大流行将这一非正式政策公之于众。根据贾斯滕的说法，解决这一差距成了“生存问题”。一旦他注意到巴西政府的网站下线了几个小时，然后又拿到了不完整的数据集，他就对巴西政府的数据产生了怀疑。该网站还花了大约两个月的时间才发布了按城市分类的数据。
贾斯滕的怀疑被证明是正确的。随着新冠肺炎感染人数呈指数级增长，新冠肺炎统计官方网站于2020年6月5日下线。当一个新版本上线时，它只显示了过去24小时的数字，累积的数据也不见了。三天后，该部被迫在最高法院的命令下恢复网站。
随着新冠肺炎数据中断的消息传开，巴西.IO的每日独立访问量从4,000人飙升至30,000人。与此同时，卫生部的紧张局势沸腾起来：与博尔索纳罗的分歧导致两名前任部长离职；反过来，总统任命了一名军队将领担任临时部长。政府还将新冠肺炎的每日新闻发布会从下午5点推迟。至晚上10点。以康复患者人数为首的保密新闻稿，被广泛认为是试图阻止他们出现在黄金时段的新闻广播和纸质报纸上。
随着戏剧性事件牵涉到行政部门，独立的数据收集者继续他们的工作。他们使用了一个信息网络，该网络将巴西的全民医疗系统与其流行病学监测部门整合在一起。当当地的医生从包括新冠肺炎在内的一系列疾病中发现一个病例时，他们会向卫生部的监测人员报告。然后，每个州都会整合这些数据，并发布每日向公众开放的流行病学公告。坎帕努奇说：“如果各州停止这样做，像巴西.IO的新冠肺炎项目这样的倡议将无法存在。”
当按预期运行时，该集成系统可对地方和联邦一级的卫生紧急情况做出快速反应。在2015年寨卡病毒流行期间，这一点至关重要，当时它发现了小头畸形症病例最初的激增，这是一种婴儿出生时头部比正常情况下更小的情况。这使得巴西的研究人员能够及早确定寨卡病毒感染和新生儿小头畸形症之间的联系。“小头畸形症只是冰山一角。巴西流行病学家韦纳·维埃拉(Wayner Vieira)说。
在开发Brasil.IO时，Justen和他的团队将这个集成系统融入到他们的工作中。在当地卫生部门的帮助下，志愿者小组覆盖了特定的州。每天，机器人都会向志愿者发送最新消息。志愿者单独工作，将最新信息上传到Brasil.IO的平台上。如果它们的数量不匹配，系统就会发出警报。在巴西这样的国家，官方统计数据中的这种差异相对常见。各国在一天中的不同时间发布流行病学公告，甚至使用不同的方法。“有时(地方政府)发布PDF文件或图片，或者数字是新闻稿的一部分，”贾斯滕告诉“世界其他地方”。“将这一过程自动化是非常困难的。”
巴西IO团队对新冠肺炎的数据封锁感到震惊，于是在推特上发布了他们的综合报告，作为联邦公告的替代方案。第一份报告是在6月6日，也就是停电后不久发布的，当时还不清楚联邦政府是否会恢复对新冠肺炎迄今收集的累积数据的访问。“我们的工作是倡导各国政府开放数据，因为这是他们的责任。独立的倡议(如Brasil.IO)之所以出现，只是因为政府未能做到这一点，“Campagnucci说。
在机器人和数十名志愿者的帮助下，截至7月11日，Brasil.IO到目前为止已经发布了36份公告。他们加入了其他组织要求提高透明度的呼声。
随着巴西新冠肺炎病例的数量继续上升-截至7月8日，已有超过170万人-甚至连总统博尔索纳罗的新冠肺炎检测也呈阳性，独立的数据收集人员还有很长的路要走。Brasil.IO继续建立数据集，现在计划研究大流行对该国的经济影响。贾斯滕说：“许多可用的信息仍然不容易被外行管理，除非他们是数据分析方面的专家。”在这种情况下，就好像数据根本不可用一样。
喜欢我们的故事吗？在Facebook、Twitter(@RestofWorld)和Instagram上关注世界其他地区，让我们知道我们在做什么。

note_en:
Like any other software developer, Álvaro Justen used to spend most of his waking hours in front of a screen, moving around in his chair, while writing code, working with databases, and processing spreadsheets. But since the first Covid-19 cases sprouted in his native Brazil, Justen’s routine changed significantly. He now works full time, without pay, on what was once a small side project that has become the comprehensive source for measurements of the pandemic’s impact in his country.
 @turicas, as colleagues and  Twitter followers know Justen, founded his nonprofit  Brasil.IO in 2018 as an initiative to make public data in Brazil more accessible. Now, he is focusing on information — or lack thereof — about the novel coronavirus in the country, one of the pandemic’s epicenters.
 “If I’m awake, I’m probably working on the site. On Saturdays, Sundays, holidays,” he told  Rest of World. He started his site by publishing lists —  candidates for public office or  business owners in the country — in a user-friendly format.
 Currently, the site’s  Covid-19 series features an independent daily monitor of cases with breakdowns by state and city. More than 40 volunteer developers and data enthusiasts work on the project, gathering updates, identifying patterns, and creating datasets.
  Collecting data can be a difficult, manual task. The volunteers work to cover more than 5,000 Brazilian cities. “Sometimes, data is only available in internal systems, and we have to request it,” Justen explained. “Or the data is available, but not in an easy format to work with.” The challenge is to turn these massive tranches of information — which can come in large spreadsheets or loose PDFs — into lighter files or user-friendly tables and graphics.
 By 2018, data transparency had come a long way in Brazil. Since the military rule ended in 1985, the country has witnessed breakthroughs like the 2011 approval of the  Access to Information Law, akin to the U.S. Freedom of Information Act.
 But experts, like Fernanda Campagnucci, executive director of  Open Knowledge Brasil, say that data transparency is not a priority for the Bolsonaro administration. Instead, it seems to prefer an unspoken policy to conceal, question, or take down any data that doesn’t agree with its narrative. “A posture toward restraining access [to information] is gaining ground, which was not typical of Brazil,” she told  Rest of World.
 These efforts began last year. In April 2019, the Bolsonaro administration took down the Brazilian Observatory on Drug Information’s website, which showed the number of illicit drug use among the population. Later, in August, the director of the  Brazilian National Institute for Space Research was fired after  coming under attack for showing that Amazon deforestation increased 68% in the first two weeks of July when compared to the same time period in the previous year. Earlier in the year, the government  cut the national census’ budget, which led to a proposal to reduce population survey questions by almost a third.
  The coronavirus pandemic threw this informal policy out in the open. Addressing this gap became a “matter of survival,” according to Justen. He grew skeptical of the numbers from the Brazilian government, once he noticed its site going offline for a few hours and coming back up with incomplete datasets. The site also took about two months to publish breakdowns by city.
 Justen’s suspicions turned out to be right. As the number of Covid-19 infections grew exponentially, the  official government website for Covid-19 statistics went offline on June 5, 2020. When a new version went live, it featured only numbers from the past 24 hours, and the cumulative data were gone. Three days later, the ministry was forced to restore the website on the orders of the Supreme Court.
    As news of the Covid-19 data  blackout spread, the number of unique daily visitors to Brasil.IO skyrocketed from 4,000 to 30,000. Meanwhile, tensions at the Ministry of Health boiled over: Disagreements with Bolsonaro had led two  previous ministers to leave their posts; the president, in turn, appointed an army general as interim minister. The government also pushed back its daily Covid-19 press briefing from 5 p.m. to 10 p.m. hush-hush  press releases that led with the numbers of recovered patients, which was widely suggested to be an attempt to stop them from being  featured in prime-time news broadcasts and print newspapers.
 As drama embroiled the executive branch, independent data collectors continued their work. They did so using an information network  that integrates Brazil’s universal healthcare system with its epidemiological surveillance department. When doctors on the ground identify a case from a list of diseases, including Covid-19, they report it to a monitor at the Ministry of Health. Each state then consolidates the data and publishes daily epidemiological bulletins that are open to the public. “If states stopped doing that, an initiative like Brasil.IO’s Covid-19 project would not be able to exist,” Campagnucci said.
 When operating as intended, this integrated system responds quickly to local- and federal-level health emergencies. It was crucial during the 2015 Zika epidemic, when it uncovered the initial surge in cases of microcephaly, a condition in which a baby is born with a smaller head than normal. That allowed researchers in Brazil to identify, early on, the link between Zika infection and microcephaly among newborns. “Microcephaly was only the tip of the iceberg. And the response to it was praiseworthy,” said Wayner Vieira, a Brazilian epidemiologist.
 While developing Brasil.IO, Justen and his team incorporated this integrated system into their work. Groups of volunteers covered specific states, with the help of local health departments. Every day, a bot sends the volunteers updates. Volunteers work separately to upload the most recent information into Brasil.IO’s platform. If their numbers don’t match, the system sends out an alert. In a country like Brazil, such differences in official statistics are relatively common. States publish epidemiological bulletins at different times of the day or even use different methodologies. “Sometimes [local governments] publish a PDF file or an image, or the numbers are part of a press release,” Justen told  Rest of World. “Automatizing this process is very hard.”
 Alarmed by the Covid-19 data blackout, the Brasil.IO team posted its consolidated reports on Twitter as an alternative to the federal bulletins. Its  first one came out on June 6, immediately after the blackout, when it was still unclear whether the federal government would restore access to the cumulative data gathered for Covid-19 thus far. “Our work is to advocate for governments to open data because this is their responsibility. Independent initiatives [like Brasil.IO] emerge only because the government fails to do so,” Campagnucci said.
 With the help of bots and dozens of volunteers, as of July 11, Brasil.IO has published 36 bulletins so far. They have joined other organizations’ calls for better transparency.
 As the number of Covid-19 cases in Brazil continues to rise — as of July 8, it was over 1,700,000 people  —  and even president Bolsonaro  tested positive for Covid-19,  independent data collectors have a long way to go. Brasil.IO continues to build datasets and now plans to research the pandemic’s economic impact in the country. “A lot of the available information still cannot be easily managed by laypeople, unless they are experts in data analysis,” Justen said. “In this case, it is as if the data was not available at all.”

“边缘摇摇欲坠”：科学家警告南极洲的“末日冰川”将迅速融化。
“独立报”在世界各地聘请记者，为您带来真正独立的新闻报道。为了支持我们，请考虑一笔额外的捐款。
许多气候科学家认为，就未来全球海平面上升而言，南极洲西部的斯韦茨冰川是世界上最脆弱、最重要的冰川之一。
它的坍塌本身将使全球海平面上升超过半米，随后释放出南极洲西部的其他主要冰体，这些冰体加起来可能会使海平面上升2-3米。这对许多国家，包括世界上大多数沿海城市来说，将是灾难性的。
正因为如此，斯韦茨被称为南极洲的“末日冰川”。
今年早些时候，一组科学家首次观察到冰川下面的一个关键点存在温水，这有助于解释冰川下降幅度背后的原因。
斯韦茨冰川面积为7.4万平方英里，大致相当于英国的面积。从Thwaites排入阿蒙森海的冰融化已经占到全球海平面上升的4%，但科学家们担心，随着世界变暖，它的持续存在正悬而未决。
“最大的问题是，它变得不稳定的速度有多快。它似乎正摇摇欲坠，“美国国家科学基金会南极冰川学项目主任保罗·卡特勒(Paul Cutler)本周告诉英国”金融时报“。
他说：“对于南极洲西部的其他冰川来说，这是一块基石。”“如果你把它移走，其他冰可能也会开始排入海洋。”
英国南极调查局Thwaites冰川项目的英国首席调查员Rob Larter补充说：“这是南极洲最脆弱的地方。”
南极洲拥有大量的冰-约占世界所有冰的90%-与北极不同的是，大多数冰都在水面外和陆地上。冰层的平均厚度为1.6英里深。在最厚的地方，冰盖几乎有3英里深。
目前的海平面比工业化前的水平高出约20厘米(几乎8英寸)，并被指责为沿海洪水增加的罪魁祸首。
在大约2000年的时间里，直到19世纪末，全球海平面几乎保持不变，只有很小的波动。
在18世纪中期的工业革命期间，化石燃料的燃烧加剧，增加了大气中二氧化碳(CO2)的水平。温室气体从太阳吸收热量并将其困住，加热大气和地球。
我们快速变暖的地球在两个方面导致海平面上升。变暖的温度融化了冰原和冰川，导致径流流入海洋。海洋还会吸收温室气体排放产生的多余热量，温水会膨胀，比冷水占据更多的空间。
科学家们之前发现很难研究南极冰盖的不稳定程度，但改进的技术使人们能够更深入地了解南极大陆及其周围的冰的变化。
今年1月，一个旨在搜索木星卫星木卫二上外星生命的探测器接受了在斯韦茨工作的科学家的测试。
这个圆柱形机器人被放入一个直径仅35厘米的600米深的洞中，以测量冰川表面下面的水流。
它测量了温度和水的湍流-一种测试来自冰川的新鲜融水和来自海洋的咸水混合的方法。这种湍流使科学家能够了解冰川融化的速度，从而了解冰川的整体稳定性。
关于南极洲冰川融化的最新警告是在地球另一边北极创纪录的热浪中发出的。
南极和北极地区的变暖速度都快于世界其他地区，但在北极，气温已经创下历史新高，西伯利亚6月份创下了38摄氏度的历史新高。
高温已引发数百起野火，俄罗斯当局已宣布七个地区进入紧急状态。

note_en:
‘Teetering at the edge’: Scientists warn of rapid melting of Antarctica’s ‘Doomsday glacier’
  The Independent employs reporters around the world to bring you truly independent journalism. To support us, please consider a  contribution.
  Many climate scientists regard  Thwaites glacier in West  Antarctica as one of the most vulnerable and most significant glaciers in the world in terms of future global  sea-level rise.
  Its collapse would raise global sea levels by more than half a metre on its own, and subsequently release other major bodies of ice in West Antarctica, which together could raise sea levels by 2-3 metres. The ramifications for many countries, including most of the world’s coastal cities, would be catastrophic.
    For this reason, Thwaites is known as Antarctica’s “Doomsday glacier”.
     Earlier this year a team of scientists observed, for the first time, the presence of warm water at a vital point underneath the glacier, which helps explain the reason behind the extent of its decrease.
         The Thwaites glacier is 74,000 square miles, roughly the size of the UK. The ice melt draining from Thwaites into the Amundsen Sea already accounts for 4 per cent of global sea-level rise but scientists are concerned its continued existence is hanging in the balance as the world warms.
      “The big question is how quickly it becomes unstable. It seems to be teetering at the edge,” Paul Cutler, programme director for Antarctic glaciology at America’s National Science Foundation  told the  Financial Times this week.
  “It is a keystone for the other glaciers around it in West Antarctica,” he said. “If you remove it, other ice will potentially start draining into the ocean too.”
    Rob Larter, UK principal investigator for the Thwaites Glacier Project at the  British Antarctic Survey, added: “It is the most vulnerable place in Antarctica.”
  Antarctica accounts for vast quantities of ice – around 90 per cent of all ice in the world - and unlike in the Arctic, most of the ice is out of the water and on land. The average thickness of the ice is 1.6 miles deep. At its thickest point, the ice sheet is almost three miles deep.
     Current sea level is around 20cm (almost 8 inches) above pre-industrial levels and is blamed for increased coastal flooding.
  For about 2,000 years until the late 1800s, global sea levels remained almost static with small fluctuations.
         The burning of fossil fuels ramped up during the Industrial Revolution in the mid-18th century, increasing levels of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the atmosphere. The greenhouse gas absorbs heat from the sun and traps it, heating the atmosphere and the planet.
     Our rapidly-warming planet causes sea levels to rise on two fronts. Warmer temperatures melt ice sheets and glaciers, leading the run-off to flow into oceans. The ocean also absorbs excess heat from greenhouse gas emissions and warm water expands, taking up more space than colder water.
    Scientists  previously found the extent of the instability of the Antarctic ice sheet difficult to study but improved technologies are allowing greater insight into the changes in the ice on, and surrounding, the continent.
  In January, a probe designed to search for alien life on Jupiter’s Moon Europa was tested by scientists working at Thwaites.
       The cylindrical robot was lowered down a 600m-deep hole just 35cm in diameter to measure the waters moving below the glacier's surface.
  It measured temperatures and also the turbulence of the water - a means of testing the mixing of fresh meltwater from the glacier and salty water from the ocean. The turbulence allows scientists to understand the rate of melting and therefore the overall stability of the glacier.
  The latest warning over Antarctica’s melting glaciers comes amid a  record heatwave on the other side of the planet in the Arctic.
  Both the Antarctic and Arctic regions are warming faster than the rest of the world but in the Arctic temperatures have hit record highs, with an alarming all-time high of 38C recorded in Siberia in June.
  The high temperatures have fuelled hundreds of wildfires and Russian authorities have declared emergency situations in seven regions.

消息人士告诉“华盛顿邮报”，周二下午，一名被斩首、被肢解的科技首席执行官的身体在下东区的一套公寓内被发现--被放在塑料袋里，就在电锯旁边。
消息人士称，下午3：30过后不久，警方发现了这一可怕的场景，此前死者的一名表亲要求对该男子位于休斯顿大街(E.Houston Street)的大楼进行福利检查，并报了警。
警方在身体附近发现一把电锯，据称是一名三十三岁男子。消息人士称，受害者膝盖以下的手臂和腿被切除，在公寓发现的塑料袋中发现了身体部位。
警方消息人士认为，受害者是风险投资家法希姆·萨利赫(Fahim Saleh)，也是尼日利亚摩托车初创公司Gokada的首席执行官。
记录显示，萨利赫被列为这套公寓的所有者，他去年以225万美元的价格购买了这套公寓。
消息人士称，受害者最后一次出现在监控录像中是在周一下午1点40分左右，当时他进入了大楼的电梯，电梯直接通向他位于七楼的公寓。
消息人士称，公寓楼的录像似乎显示，嫌疑杀手提着一个包，等待与受害者一起进入电梯。
在一次交流中，受害者用困惑的目光看着嫌疑人，因为他们一起骑马过来。据消息人士称，受害者一走进公寓，就可以看到嫌疑人袭击他。
一名执法部门消息人士称，犯罪现场看起来像是一起“职业”杀人事件，因为几乎没有留下任何血迹，公寓也没有被洗劫一空。
而另一位消息人士补充说，看起来凶手被“打断”了。
这位消息人士说：“看起来罪犯还没做完。”
调查人员认为受害者是萨利赫，但他们正在等待法医确认他的身份。
后来有人看到警察在大楼的大堂审问一名哭泣的妇女-消息来源称这名女子是受害者的表妹。
据消息人士透露，受害者的表妹发现他的躯干暴露在客厅里的建筑袋旁边，并在周二没有听到他的消息后去看他时看到了这一点。

“她真的很难过。哭啊。颤抖，“当地居民丹尼·浮士德说。“她只是坐在那里，但你可以看出她的腿在发抖。她很紧张。她哭得就像，你知道的，擦着眼睛。“
他说，他看到警察聚集在现场后，就去了大楼。
“”死尸？好的，“他说。“但是当你听到被切碎和肢解的声音时呢？”就这样。有人这样做是一种病态的心态。“。
萨利赫是孟加拉裔美国人，根据他的LinkedIn页面，他创立并投资了几家与交通相关的初创企业。
2015年，他与人共同创立了Pathao，这是一家位于他父母的祖国孟加拉国的叫车公司。
大约两年后，也就是2017年12月，他在尼日利亚拉各斯与人共同创立了Gokada，这是一家按需叫车公司，并于去年4月接任首席执行官一职。他最近在冠状病毒大流行期间监督了公司向送货服务的过渡。
早在2018年，他还在纽约成立了风险投资公司Adenture Capital。
“我们过去称他为发展中世界的埃隆·马斯克。他就像能量兔，“一位老朋友告诉”华盛顿邮报“。
“他在迪拜最好的朋友打电话给我，只是在电话里抽泣着说，‘他们带走了我的儿子；他们带走了我的儿子！’他演奏了尤克里莱…。他的想法太丰富了，“这位朋友补充道。
居民们说，这起犯罪特别令人不安，因为它发生在一个通常是安静的社区。
“这令人震惊。这太可怕了，“45岁的居民杰森·里维拉告诉”华盛顿邮报“。“一个人的脑海里怎么会有斩首的念头呢？”纯粹的邪恶。“
note_en:
The body of a decapitated, dismembered tech CEO was found inside a Lower East Side apartment on Tuesday afternoon — sorted in plastic bags right next to a power saw, sources told The Post.
 Police came upon the grisly scene shortly after 3:30 p.m., after a cousin of the dead man requested a welfare check at the man’s E. Houston Street building and called police, the sources said.
 An electric saw was found near the body, which was described as a 33-year-old man. The victim’s arms and legs below the knees were removed, and body parts were found in plastic bags found in the apartment, the sources said.
 Police sources believe the victim to be Fahim Saleh, a venture capitalist and the CEO of the Nigeria-based motorbike startup Gokada.
 Saleh is listed as the owner of the apartment, which he purchased last year for $2.25 million, records show.
    The victim was last seen on surveillance footage Monday afternoon around 1:40 p.m., when he entered his building’s elevator, which opens right out to his seventh-floor apartment, sources said.
 The footage from the apartment building appears to show the suspected killer carrying a bag, waiting to enter the elevator with the victim, sources said.
 The victim gives the suspect a puzzled look during an exchange as they ride up together. As soon as the victim steps out into his apartment, the suspect can be seen attacking him, according to sources.
 One law enforcement source said the crime scene looked like a “professional” killing because almost no blood was left behind and the apartment was not looted.
 While another source added that it appeared that the killer had been “interrupted.”
 “It didn’t look like the perp was done,” the source said.
 Investigators believe the victim to be Saleh, but they’re waiting for the Medical Examiner to confirm his identity.
 Police were later seen questioning a sobbing woman — who sources identified as the victim’s cousin — in the lobby of the building.
 The victim’s cousin found his torso exposed in the living room alongside the construction bags and saw when she went to check on him Tuesday after not hearing from him, according to sources.
  
 “She was really upset. Crying. Shaking,” local resident Danny Faust said. “She was just sitting there but you can tell her legs were shaking. She’s nervous. She was crying like, you know, wiping her eyes.”
  He said he went over to the building after seeing police converge on the scene.
 “Dead body? Okay,” he said. “But when you hear chopped up and dismembered? That’s it. That’s a sick type of mind for somebody to do that.”
 Saleh was a Bangladeshi-American who founded and invested in several transportation-related start-ups, according to his LinkedIn page.
 In 2015, he co-founded Pathao, a ride-hailing company in his parents’ home country of Bangladesh.
 Roughly two years later, in December 2017, he co-founded Gokada, an on-demand, motorbike-hailing company in Lagos, Nigeria, and took over as its CEO in April of last year. He most recently oversaw the company’s transition to delivery service during the coronavirus pandemic.
 He also launched the venture capital firm Adventure Capital in New York back in 2018.
 “We used to call him the Elon Musk of the developing world. He was like the Energizer bunny,” a longtime friend told The Post.
 “His best friend in Dubai called me just sobbing on the phone, ‘They took my boy; they took my boy!’ He played ukelele…he was so full of ideas,” the friend added.
 Residents said the crime was particularly unsettling because it took place in what is typically a quiet neighborhood.
 “It’s shocking. It’s gruesome,” resident Jason Rivera, 45, told The Post. “What could possibly be going through someone’s mind to decapitate him? Pure evilness.”
title: 在纽约公寓发现被斩首、肢解的科技CEO身体
title_en: Decapitated, dismembered body of tech CEO found in NYC apartment

note:
根据新闻声明，“委员会没有成功地向必要的法律标准表明，就”欧洲联盟运作条约“(TFEU)第107(1)条而言是有利的。”
早在2017年，欧盟委员会就表示，苹果接受了非法的国家援助，应该缴纳更多税款。但欧洲一审法院总法院表示，这一论点并不代表法律依据。
法院在一份声明中写道：“根据总法院的说法，欧盟委员会宣布(苹果国际销售公司)和(苹果欧洲运营公司)获得了选择性的经济优势，进而获得了国家援助，这是错误的。”
今天的决定是对欧盟委员会(European Commission)追踪跨国公司的战略的打击，这些跨国公司一直在优化税收结构，以降低整个欧洲的有效税率-这一战略主要由时任竞争事务专员玛格丽特·维斯特格(Margrethe Vestager)体现。
2003年至2014年间，苹果在欧洲与两家主要子公司合作-苹果销售国际公司和苹果运营欧洲公司。当时，欧盟委员会表示，这些子公司将绝大多数利润归因于只存在于纸面上的总部。维斯特格在2016年写道：“这种有选择性的待遇使苹果2003年为其欧洲利润支付1%的有效企业税率降至2014年的0.005%。”
苹果的论据一直都相当直截了当。根据该公司的说法，爱尔兰从未与苹果达成协议。8月30日发表的意见称，爱尔兰在我们的税收问题上给予苹果特殊待遇。这种说法既没有事实根据，也没有法律依据。我们从未要求过，也没有收到过任何特殊交易，“苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克在2016年表示。
虽然苹果一直坚称它遵守欧洲的税法，但它利用了爱尔兰优惠的税法和所谓的双重爱尔兰税收结构。
随着税收优化方案的来来去去，苹果在2014年改变了其欧洲结构。苹果国际销售公司(Apple Sales International)和苹果运营国际公司(Apple Operations International)将现金储备转移到了小岛泽西岛。
2018年，苹果开始拨款，以防不得不向爱尔兰偿还130亿欧元。所有东西目前都在第三方托管账户中。败诉方仍然可以就法律问题对决定提出上诉，因此这笔钱可能会在第三方托管账户中保留一段时间。
note_en:
According to  the press statement, “the Commission did not succeed in showing to the requisite legal standard that there was an advantage for the purposes of Article 107(1) TFEU [Treaty of the Functioning of the European Union].”
 Back in 2017, the Commission said Apple received illegal state aid and should have paid more taxes. But the General Court, Europe’s first instance court, says that this argument doesn’t represent a legal basis.
  “According to the General Court, the Commission was wrong to declare that [Apple Sales International] and [Apple Operations Europe] had been granted a selective economic advantage and, by extension, State aid,” the court  wrote in a statement.
 Today’s decision represents a blow to the  European Commission’s   strategy to track down multinational corporations that have been optimizing their tax structure in order to lower their effective tax rate across Europe — a strategy that was mostly incarnated by then Competition Commissioner Margrethe Vestager.
 Between 2003 and 2014, Apple operated with two main subsidiaries in Europe — Apple Sales International and Apple Operations Europe. Back then, the Commission said those subsidiaries attributed the vast majority of their profit to a head office that only exists on paper. “This selective treatment allowed Apple to pay an effective corporate tax rate of 1 per cent on its European profits in 2003 down to 0.005 per cent in 2014,” Vestager  wrote in 2016.
 Apple’s arguments have always been quite straightforward. According to the company, Ireland never cut a deal with Apple. “The opinion issued on August 30th alleges that Ireland gave Apple a special deal on our taxes. This claim has no basis in fact or in law. We never asked for, nor did we receive, any special deals,” Apple CEO Tim Cook  said in 2016.
 While Apple has continuously maintained that it complies with tax laws in Europe, it took advantage of favorable tax laws in Ireland and the so-called  Double Irish tax structure.
 As tax optimization schemes come and go, Apple changed its European structure in 2014. Apple Sales International and Apple Operations International  moved its cash stockpile to the tiny island of Jersey.
 In 2018, Apple started allocating money in case it had to pay back €13 billion to Ireland. Everything is currently sitting in an  escrow account. The defeated side can still appeal the decision on points of law, so the money might remain in the escrow account a little longer.
title: 苹果和爱尔兰赢得针对欧盟执委会150亿美元税收裁决的上诉
title_en: Apple and Ireland win appeal against the European Commission’s $15 billion tax ruling

note:
苹果公司周三赢得了针对欧盟执委会的法庭诉讼，该案件涉及约130亿欧元(149亿美元)的爱尔兰税收纠纷。
在一项备受期待的里程碑式裁决中，欧盟总法院裁定，欧盟委员会未能成功证明爱尔兰政府给予这家美国科技巨头的绝对优势。
欧盟执行机构欧盟委员会在2016年8月已经得出结论，爱尔兰政府向苹果公司提供了非法的税收优惠，并命令其追回130亿欧元。
当时，欧盟委员会表示，多年来，爱尔兰让苹果公司缴纳的税款比其他企业少得多，这意味着这家美国公司2003年获准为其欧洲利润支付1%的有效公司税率，而2014年这一税率降至0.005%。
爱尔兰政府和苹果公司决定对欧盟委员会的决定提出上诉，后者争辩说，退税命令违背了现实和常识。
爱尔兰、苹果和欧盟委员会(European Commission)现在有两个月的时间来决定是否要对最新的法院裁决提出上诉，并可能将其提交欧盟最高法庭。
作为对法院裁决的回应，爱尔兰政府周三表示，一直以来都很清楚，没有向这两家苹果公司提供特殊待遇，正确的爱尔兰税额是按照爱尔兰正常的税收规则征收的。
CNBC周三上午联系到欧盟委员会(European Commission)和苹果公司请其置评。消息传出后，苹果股价在盘前交易中上涨约1.2%。
这起涉及一家美国大型科技公司的案件尤其重要，也是欧盟近年来打击税收的核心。这可能会影响布鲁塞尔机构在税收问题上与其他公司打交道的方式。
在新冠肺炎危机之后，税收的作用更加突出。随着许多政府加大支出，他们将以税收的形式寻找新的收入来源。
在这种背景下，关于欧盟是否应该有自己的数字税收制度-对大型科技巨头征税，以确保它们与更传统的企业相比支付更公平的份额-的辩论仍在进行中。
西班牙外交大臣阿兰查·冈萨雷斯(Arancha González)周三在接受CNBC“Squawk Box”采访时表示：无论这些公司是美国公司、中国公司、日本公司、韩国公司还是欧洲公司，这都事关税收制度的公平。
包括西班牙在内的一些欧洲国家对这些科技巨头或更多公司征税的计划遭到了美国的反对，美国认为这项税收对其国内公司是歧视性的。
我们要说的是，公平要求每一项经济活动，无论经济活动是以模拟方式提供还是以数字方式提供，以贡献其公平的税收份额，西班牙部长补充道。
note_en:
Apple won a court case Wednesday against the European Commission over a dispute concerning 13 billion euros ($14.9 billion) in Irish taxes.
 In a highly-anticipated landmark decision, the EU's general court decided that the European Commission did not succeed in proving that there was an advantage given by the Irish government to the U.S. tech giant.
  The Commission, the executive arm of the EU, had  concluded in  August 2016 that the Irish government granted illegal tax benefits to Apple and ordered it to recover 13 billion euros.
 At the time, the Commission said that Ireland had enabled Apple to pay "substantially less tax than other businesses over many years," which meant that the U.S. firm  was allowed to pay an effective corporate tax rate of 1% on its European profits in 2003, which fell to 0.005% in 2014.
 The Irish government and Apple decided to appeal the Commission's decision, with the latter arguing the order to repay taxes "defies reality and common sense."
 Ireland, Apple and the European Commission now have two months to decide if they want to appeal the latest court ruling and potentially take it to the EU's highest tribunal.
 In reaction to the court ruling, the Irish government said Wednesday that it has always been clear "that there was no special treatment provided to the two Apple companies" and that "the correct amount of Irish tax was charged taxation in line with normal Irish taxation rules."
  The European Commission and Apple were not immediately available for comment when contacted by CNBC Wednesday morning. Apple shares were up around 1.2% in pre-market trade on the news.
   This case, involving a giant U.S. tech firm, is particularly important and a centerpiece of the EU's crackdown on taxation in recent years. It could impact how the Brussels institution deals with other companies over taxation matters.
 Taxation is taking an even more prominent role in the wake of the Covid-19 crisis. With many governments stepping up their spending, they will be looking for new sources of revenue in the form of taxation.
 In this context, there's an ongoing debate as to whether the European Union should have its own digital tax — a levy on big tech giants to ensure they pay a fairer share compared to more traditional businesses.
 Arancha González, minister of foreign affairs for Spain, told CNBC's "Squawk Box Wednesday": "Whether the companies are American, whether they are Chinese, Japanese, Korean or European, this is about fairness of taxation systems."
 Plans by some European nations, including Spain, to tax the technology behemoths more have met opposition from the United States, which argues the levy is discriminatory toward its domestic firms.
 "What we are saying is that fairness requires every economic activity, whether the economic activity is provided analogically or digitally to contribute with their fair share of taxes," the Spanish minister added.
title: 苹果因130亿欧元未缴税款赢得欧盟法院支持
title_en: Apple wins backing of EU court over 13B euros in unpaid taxes

note_en:
Users in the U.S. can now buy, hold and sell Bitcoin and Ethereum from the  Revolut   app. The feature is going to be available in 49 states as there are some regulatory issues in Tennessee. If you have USD or other currencies in your Revolut account, you can exchange manually whenever you want.
 You can also set up alerts in case there are some important price changes happening. Optionally, users can also round up card payments to the nearest whole dollar and convert spare change into crypto assets.
  If you’re familiar with Revolut’s cryptocurrency feature, you know that the company gives you access to more cryptocurrencies in Europe, such as Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash and XRP. The company says it is starting with BTC and ETH in the U.S. but is already working on bringing more cryptocurrencies.
 When it comes to fees, users with a free Revolut account will pay 2.5% in conversion fees. Users with a Premium and Metal subscription will pay 1.5% in fees. Revolut is waving fees for the first 30 days.
 This is in line with the company’s  current fees in Europe. Revolut also has some monthly limits on currency exchange in general for free users as well — it can be fiat currencies or cryptocurrencies. You have to pay a 0.5% fee above that limit or pay for a subscription.
 Revolut made some changes to its cryptocurrency feature  recently. While you now technically own your cryptocurrencies, you can’t send and receive cryptocurrencies from third-party wallets. The feature is all about trading — buying, holding and selling.
 In the U.S., Square’s Cash App and Robinhood also let you buy cryptocurrencies in their respective app. While those features don’t offer the same flexibility as a full-fledged cryptocurrency exchange, it makes it easy to get started with cryptocurrencies.
